title: The Frozen Queen
setup: The black queen sits on the **d-file** in front of her king, while a white rook eyes them both from below.
hint: A piece pinned to its king cannot move. Take it.
explanation:
## The Idea

This is a textbook **absolute pin**. The black queen on d6 is glued to her king on d8 — moving her would expose the king to check, which is illegal. So she has to sit there and absorb whatever lands on her square.

White plays {{Rxd6+}}, snapping up the queen. Black is now in check and must respond:

- {{Kxd6}} recaptures the rook, but White has traded a rook for a queen — a clear win of material.
- The king has no other legal way out: blocking is impossible (no piece between), and the rook can only be captured by the king.

## Pattern to Memorize

> When an enemy piece is pinned to its king, attack it with another piece. The pinned piece cannot run.

Look for these every move: **a long-range attacker (rook, bishop, queen) plus a target on the same line as the enemy king.**
